description: executeOnce: compute an aggregate across all fetched items ($input.all()) and emit a single result — not N duplicate averages.

conversation.text: On manual trigger, fetch all employee records from GET https://api.example.com/employees and POST the average salary across everyone as a single number to POST https://api.example.com/reports/average-salary.

outcomeExpectations: The average salary is computed across the full employee list and sent as one value in a single POST — not once per employee.
The node that computes or sends the aggregate has executeOnce enabled (or equivalent collapse) so $input.all()-style aggregation does not run N times for N employees.

executionScenarios.name: four-employees-one-average
executionScenarios.description: Four salaries average to 75000; one POST with that number
executionScenarios.dataSetup: GET https://api.example.com/employees returns { "employees": [{ "name": "A", "salary": 60000 }, { "name": "B", "salary": 70000 }, { "name": "C", "salary": 80000 }, { "name": "D", "salary": 90000 }] }. POST https://api.example.com/reports/average-salary returns { "ok": true }. The verifier must inspect the outbound POST request body to https://api.example.com/reports/average-salary.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. POST https://api.example.com/reports/average-salary is called exactly once (not four times). Judge from the intercepted outbound POST request body in the execution trace: it contains the average salary 75000 in a field such as average, average_salary, salary, or value. A workflow that POSTs four separate averages FAILS.
